verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO (Izumo sperm and egg fusogenic protein) family. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is 'located in nucleus,' indicating primary nuclear localization. The IZUMO family members are involved in reproductive cell fusion (sperm-egg interaction), but IZUMO4 itself has not been documented as a surface-accessible protein in published evidence. Cross-checking against contextual scenarios: (1) no cell-state induction to surface documented; (2) tissue restriction to germline is possible but the protein is stated nuclear, not surface-presented; (3) no lysosomal exocytosis mechanism reported; (4) no dual localization with PM residence documented; (5) no stable surface-attachment mechanism described. The canonical biology of IZUMO family proteins (IZUMO1, IZUMO2/JUNO, IZUMO3) centers on membrane-anchored fusogenic machinery during spermatogenesis and fertilization, but IZUMO4's explicit nuclear annotation and lack of surface biological evidence place it as intracellular. No binder modality literature or surface proteomics evidence supports extracellular accessibility.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO family members are reproductive and fusogenic proteins; IZUMO1/JUNO are well-characterized as sperm/egg surface proteins. IZUMO4's exact role in the IZUMO pathway and whether it undergoes any transient PM trafficking during gametogenesis is not fully established from the provided evidence. Nuclear annotation is explicit but reproductive proteins can have complex subcellular dynamics.
